semantic_description: array_max scans a non-empty integer array a[0..n-1] without modifying memory. It initializes ret from a[0], then uses a for loop from i = 1 to n - 1 to keep ret equal to an element of the already-scanned prefix and greater than or equal to every element in that prefix. On return, ret is an element of the whole input list l and every valid array element is less than or equal to ret. The loop may be skipped when n == 1, and the IntArray::full heap predicate must be preserved unchanged.
